item.title: Images of Note: Glong
item.description:
There's like, so many fucking stoner films out there. ...I kinda wanna make a stoner film.
Mr. Hands
I love how they agree that TOS and Kelvin Chekov would have two different responses, unless that's mirror Chekov.
http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii Choose wisely
I will make this picture.
Clint's Goon cave
Amazing
If you do it slowly, they feel everything.
:)
Blaps
My great Grandmother used to scream insults at her Daughter in law from the street. Like her son was off fighting in World War Two and she would walk like, two miles to my great aunt's place and just scream horrible insults at her from the street. I don't know much about my Great Great Grandmother other than her sirname.
Love how Edward Cullen looks absolutely zooted.
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
It would be fuckin' baller.
Crash Bandicoot isn't from Florida, he's Australian, he's likely from Tazzie.
:)
No thoughts
Oh my my
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay also TWITCHY POOS: http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii Also also HAPPY MAY DAY, SACRIFICE A CEO TO THE GODDESS OF SPRING!
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: Effective Grandmother
item.description: You're going down evil broccoli!! Just hoping to help you smile or mentally smirk today, even if only for a moment. Cheers!!

item.title: Bath routine for the best boy
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Hilarious Wednesday meme dump of pure chaos
item.description:
1. You see this girl in a bar. What do you do? GO!
2
3. What do we say to the god of death?
5. Stop motion Mario
6. The prosecution rests, your Honor
7
8. Be careful what you wish for
9
10. Who doesn't like THICC moms
11
12. Did this guy just invent a new sport?
13. Side question: would you let her destroy you?
14. Would you like to know more?
15. Thank you for making it to the end of this dump, whole you were thoroughly entertained! I would like to share with you that I'm making a wacky game called Do Not Press The Button, and it's tone and style are kind of inspired by the Stanley Parable. But there are lots of stuff to do in there, for example JOINING THE CHAIR REVOLUTION! If you liked the humor in this dump, you would probably like the game. Please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. And happy International Workers' Day (or Labour Day) to the European homies!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: A Dad's love for his kid ....
item.description: This struck me close to home for me. https://imgur.com/gallery/rWuaM7O I kept the last voicemail my dad ever left for me and my son on the house phone. I have since recorded it to my computer, but every now and then I'll just go back and hit the play button to hear his voice, one more time. He was a good dad and grandpa! Miss you dad. Please tell your loved ones you love them.
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: And it's a role I'm excited about!
item.description: Been a long 14 months. I came out of IT and laid off during the half million+ IT layoffs in the last 24 months. Market competition has been extremely stiff. Now I'm in a Director role in the industry my masters is in specifically. So damn excited!

item.dc:creator: xbadxfaeriex

item.title: i dont know what I'm feeling
item.description:
Truck + bike collision, I was the third or so person to help, one on the phone, one on the tourniquet, me to talk to him and keep him from looking at his limbs.
It was described as a "horror scene", when I saw all the blood I pulled over, I didn't even think, I just went and went and went. Then I helped the fire fighters, the ambos, and the police before I left. Then I cried a lot in the car after all the services arrived, because shock
I still feel all over the place, but I'm no longer so overwhelmed. I wouldn't change jumping in, and I would do it again in an instant. Anyway, if anyone has any tips on coping with the stress from an event like that, let me know MV edit: This community is amazing, I really appreciate everyone's comments, support, advice, and for sharing your own stories. It has helped me feel a bit more normal. My dad (who's a longhaul truck driver) has had to be the first and sometimes only help to someone before calling emergency services, and he said the first time is always a bit brutal. I am still going to work today (because I actually adore my job, and it's just doing a science show for a school in walking distance from my house) but I am going to have a 4 day weekend and play some Stardew Valley and spend time with my husband. I'll book a drs appointment asap and have counselling scheduled for the weekend (my psych works from home and was happy to move my appointment up, shes amazing and I feel very safe with her). But all the management at my job (a new job, not one in my old posts) have been amazing and have reached out. They understand if I need time or space or anything at all, and I feel like I have an amazing network of professionals, family, and colleagues, so I feel very lucky compared to most. Hug your loved ones, look out for motorcyclists, and please if you get on a motorcycle, wear riders gear. I know its scorching hot some days in Australia - and elsewhere in the world too, but shorts do not protect your legs.
item.dc:creator: FluroShoes

item.title: Old school off-roading
item.description: First held in 1939, the Welsh trial is organized yearly by The Bulletin of the VSCC. Tons of old vintage cars drive to the town of Presteigne to plough through the greasy trails on the hills of the Welsh countryside.

item.title: Never forget Dobri
item.description: Dobri Dobrev, a man who lost his hearing in the second world war, walked 10 kilometers from his village in his homemade clothes to the city of Sofia, where he spent the day begging for money. Though a well known fixture around several of the city’s churches, known for his prostrations of thanks to all donors, it was discovered that he donated every penny he collected — over 40,000 euros — towards the restoration of decaying bulgarian monasteries and churches and the utility bills of orphanages, living instead off his monthly state pension of 80 euros. He passed away in 2018. https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dobri_Dobrev
item.dc:creator: chickengood

item.title: My former employer is going out of business V2
item.description: The job I worked from March 2018 to October 2023 was a wonderful learning experience and helped me find out more about my talents and abilities. But my former employer abused me, marginalized my work, and lied to me at every turn. We became uncommonly successful as a business during Covid, and the success continued but somehow the money was disappearing faster than it was coming in. In 2022 I was scheduling work of 3 sides of the company, doing the work of 2, and managing a warehouse filled with product rolling in, and scheduling the work as efficiently and functionally as I could. I worked an average of 55 hours a week just to keep it all moving forward. In 2023 most of my best guys had left, because of the shitty things the boss/owner was saying when I wasn't around. Lots of lies and false promises. Despite 2022 being as profitable as 2021, ($2.3m to $2.4m in sales) by 2023 we were broke, he had allowed our health insurance to lapse due to non-payment, I wasn't getting reimbursed for using my personal cell phone for work, and the product quality had dipped considerably. For the betterment of my health and well-being I left, and was unemployed for 3 months before securing a lower pay corporate job with actual benefits. I just found out that his main - and only sales guy - left last Friday, the work crews are all planning to leave soon, he's almost $700k in debt, he's had to sell multiple company vehicles, and he won't talk to anyone. I'm glad he's going under. He had a great crew, superb sales people, a fantastic company model, and a solid 5 star Google rating to back up the work. His greed has destroyed everything. Special edit: https://imgur.com/gallery/sl7gBN0 <---- My original post.
item.dc:creator: erikleorga

item.title: It's time for another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
well i need that now
what am I, Liam Neeson?
don't talk to me or my sons ever again
mmm hmm
spelling can be tough too
yes but in a higher pitch obviously
hero or villain?
whatever those things are called
makes sense
good to know
buck up, Maneson. things will be ok
this meme is really bad.. that's why i like it
so hard to find a new Mist-Pak
Greg probably
exciting
I wish
that’s all for me, have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7
